item.title: SES 2025: Strain-gradient effects and polarization in soft materials
item.dc:creator: Berkin Dortdivanlioglu
item.description: Presented two talks at the Society of Engineering Science (SES) 2025 Conference in Atlanta, GA: B. Dortdivanlioglu, J. Wang, A. Umurhan — A Micromechanical, Coupled Mechanics Approach to Strain-Gradient-Driven Polarization in Soft Polymers J. Wang, B. Dortdivanlioglu — Size Effects in Soft Material Instabilities Using a Strain-Gradient Approach Both presentations...

item.title: USNCCM18: Strain-gradient soft mechanics and an MPM scheme
item.dc:creator: Berkin Dortdivanlioglu
item.description: Presented two talks at the 18th U.S. National Congress on Computational Mechanics (USNCCM18) in Chicago, IL: B. Dortdivanlioglu, J. Wang — Strain-Gradient Effects on Instabilities in Soft Materials at Small Scales A. A. Madadi, B. Dortdivanlioglu — A Nitsche-Enhanced Extended B-Spline Mixed Material Point Method for Coupled Problems in Soft...

item.title: EMI 2025: Size effects in soft materials
item.dc:creator: Berkin Dortdivanlioglu
item.description: Gave a talk at the Engineering Mechanics Institute Conference (EMI) 2025 in Anaheim, CA: B. Dortdivanlioglu, J. Wang — Size-Dependent Instabilities in Highly Deformable Strain-Gradient Materials The work behind this talk is led by PhD student Jun and develops a strain-gradient framework for predicting how length-scale effects shift the onset...

item.title: Editor of the ASME Mechanics of Soft Materials Newsletter
item.dc:creator: bd24583
item.description: Berkin has been elected as the new editor of the ASME (Applied Mechanics Division) Technical Committee on Mechanics of Soft Materials. According to the policy, he will continue to serve the committee as secretary, then as vice chair, and finally as chair over the next four years.

item.title: NSF CAREER Award
item.dc:creator: Berkin Dortdivanlioglu
item.description: The National Science Foundation has selected our group’s proposal CAREER: Multiscale Mechanics of Highly Deformable Strain-Gradient Electric Generators for the NSF CAREER Award. The project develops theory and simulation tools to understand how strain-gradient effects, finite deformation, and electromechanical coupling come together in soft polymeric generators — and how those...

item.title: Kolodzey Travel Award
item.dc:creator: jw55692
item.description: Ashkan received the Kolodzey Travel Award from the CAEE department at UT Austin, which allowed him to attend the MPM Workshop in Berkeley, CA, where he presented his research on Mechanics of Polymeric Porous Membranes using a Stabilized Extended B-Spline Material Point Method.

item.title: 15th Annual MPM Workshop (UC Berkeley): Porous polymeric membranes via stabilized extended B-spline MPM
item.dc:creator: jw55692
item.description: Ashkan attended the 15th Annual MPM Workshop at the University of California, Berkeley, held from September 5-6, 2024. He gave a presentation titled Mechanics of Porous Polymeric Membranes Using Stabilized Extended B-spline Material Point Method. Well done, Ashkan!

item.title: George J. Heuer, Jr. Endowed Fellowship awarded to three lab Ph.D. students
item.dc:creator: jw55692
item.description: Three of our lab’s Ph.D. students—Ashkan, Ekim, and Jun—have been awarded the George J. Heuer, Jr. Ph.D. Endowed Fellowship from the Cockrell School of Engineering for the 2024-2025 academic year. This fellowship recognizes and supports their outstanding research and contributions in their fields.

item.title: EMI 2024: Stabilized extended B-spline MPM for multi-field soft materials with Nitsche BCs
item.dc:creator: jw55692
item.description: Ashkan, a Ph.D. student in our lab, presented his research titled “Stabilized Extended B-Spline Material Point Method for Multi-Field Soft Materials with Nitsche Imposition of Boundary Conditions” at the Engineering Mechanics Institute Conference (EMI 2024). The conference was held in Chicago from May 28 to 31. Congratulations to Ashkan!

item.title: Fracture of Soft Materials Symposium: Modeling nearly incompressible behavior in soft solids with MPM
item.dc:creator: jw55692
item.description: Ashkan and Ekim, Ph.D. students in our lab, presented their collaborative research titled “Modeling Nearly Incompressible Behavior in Soft Solids Using the Material Point Method” at the Fracture of Soft Materials Symposium, held in Austin from May 12 to 16, 2024. Their work, co-authored with Richard, was showcased during the...

item.title: CAEE 2024 Faculty Teaching Award
item.dc:creator: jw55692
item.description: Berkin is the 2024 recipient of CAEE’s Faculty Teaching Award! Nominated by CAEE students and selected by a committee of previous award recipients.

item.title: APS March Meeting 2024: Diffusion-induced swelling of shape morphing hydrogels
item.dc:creator: jw55692
item.description: Ashkan, a Ph.D. student in our lab, presented his research titled “Diffusion-Induced Swelling of Shape Morphing Hydrogels” at the American Physical Society (APS) March Meeting 2024. The conference took place in Minneapolis from March 3 to 8. Congratulations to Ashkan!

item.title: End-of-year gathering, 2023
item.dc:creator: jw55692
item.description:
Our group’s end-of-year gathering, December 2023.
(left to right) Zahra, Ashkan, Ekim, Derya, BD, Richard, Dat, Jun, Seda

item.title: SES 2023 (Minneapolis)
item.dc:creator: jw55692
item.description: Minneapolis, October 8-11, 2023 PhD students, Ashkan, Derya, Jun, and Ekim, participated in the 2023 Society of Engineering Science Annual Technical Meeting in Minneapolis. Ashkan presented on “a subdivision-stabilized isogeometric material point method for strongly coupled diffusion in hydrogels, a new Eulerian-Lagrangian formulation for highly deformable materials with diffusion. Ashkan...

item.title: CAEE renamed: Fariborz Maseeh Department of Civil, Architectural, and Environmental Engineering
item.dc:creator: jw55692
item.description: We attended the renaming ceremony at UT Austin.

item.title: USNCCM17: Stabilized MPM for porous hydrogels and viscoelasto-capillary soft materials
item.dc:creator: jw55692
item.description:
We presented our research at the 17th U.S. National Congress on Computational Mechanics (USNCCM) held in Albuquerque, New Mexico.
Ashkan gave a presentation titled “Stabilized Material Point Method for Self-Contacting Porous Hydrogels,” and Berkin talked about modeling viscoelasto-capillary deformations in soft materials.

item.title: EMI 2023: B-spline MPM for poroelastic materials and adhesive–capillary forces in soft materials
item.dc:creator: jw55692
item.description: We attended the Engineering Mechanics Institute Conference (EMI) held in Atlanta, Georgia in June 2023. Ashkan presented his work on “B-spline Material Point Method for Strongly Coupled Poroelastic Materials,” and Berkin talked about modeling adhesive and capillary forces in soft materials.

item.title: Dr. Marco Antonio Zuniga Izaguirre Endowed Scholarship
item.dc:creator: jw55692
item.description:
Richard has been selected as the recipient of the prestigious Dr. Marco Antonio Zuniga Izaguirre Endowed Scholarship in Engineering and Physics.
Congrats, Richard on this well-deserved recognition!

item.title: 2023 Ivo & Renata Babuska Scholarship in CSE
item.dc:creator: jw55692
item.description: Richard has been awarded the 2023 Ivo & Renata Babuska Scholarship in Computational Science and Engineering. This award was established to recognize and support the studies of exceptional senior-level undergraduate students at the university who are undertaking research at the Oden Institute for Computational Engineering and Sciences.

item.title: Best Presentation, 4th CAEE Graduate Symposium
item.dc:creator: jw55692
item.description: Derya received the first-place prize for best presentation in the 4th CAEE Graduate Symposium held at UT Austin on March 24, with her presentation on modeling the adhesive interfaces between soft materials.
